articles.title: What is 'Dharavi Didi'? Adani launches AI-powered chatbot to provide verified information on Dharavi redevelopment
articles.summary: Adani Navbharat Developers has launched Dharavi Didi, an AI companion for verified project information. The platform is meant to improve access, transparency and trust for Dharavikars during redevelopment.

articles.title: DDA's biggest housing deal of 2026: Ready-to-move freehold flats in Delhi at 25% off, near upcoming Metro — step by step guide
articles.summary: DDA Karmajeevi Awaas Yojana 2026 offers 1,224 ready-to-move flats in Narela at 25% off. Registration opens July 24. Booking from August 15 on first come, first served basis.

articles.title: AI-led data centre boom is boosting fibre optic cable demand, says HFCL MD Mahendra Nahata
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence is emerging as a key driver of global demand for fibre optic cables, with hyperscale data centre expansion boosting orders worldwide.

articles.title: India's new road trip map: Mumbai-Pune busiest road trip route but Tier-2 routes are growing fastest - Report
articles.summary: Mumbai to Pune, Delhi to Agra, Bengaluru to Mysuru, Lucknow to Kanpur, and Ahmedabad to Vadodara remained India's highest-volume intercity corridors during the quarter

articles.title: Fed up with Noida traffic? This Metro extension could save you time if approved today
articles.summary: The 7.5‑km Aqua Line extension with five new stations from Sector‑61 to Greater Noida West Sector‑4 (Kisan Chowk) is expected to be built in about two years at an estimated cost of around ₹1000 crore.

articles.title: Why Palm Jumeirah remains recognisable from space after two decades
articles.summary: Satellite and astronaut images reveal how Palm Jumeirah’s 17 fronds, 11km crescent and vast reclaimed footprint transformed Dubai’s coastline into an orbital landmark.

articles.title: Adani Group approaches govt to relax airline-operating norms: Could it take on IndiGo, Air India?
articles.summary: Move comes as the civil aviation ministry mulls breaking the airline duopoly in India

articles.title: Zepto wins interim trademark battle as Delhi High Court restrains NBFC's use of brand name
articles.summary: The High Court prohibited the defendants from using the marks "Zepto" and "Zepto Finance" until further orders

articles.title: Travelling abroad with kids? This ₹10 lakh ruling against Qatar Airways could protect your rights
articles.summary: Roshan Jose, an immigrant residing in Italy, had travelled with his wife Vinaya and their two children from Venice to Kochi via Doha in 2018 using Qatar Airways

articles.title: Rupee rises to 96.49 against US dollar, rising oil prices limit gains
articles.summary: The Indian rupee saw a slight gain against the US dollar on Thursday. Rising crude oil prices continue to exert pressure on the currency. The Reserve Bank of India's intervention is expected to limit sharper losses.

articles.title: More pulp, less tax: Govt panel hits the sweet spot with 'aam' proposal to help Totapuri farmers
articles.summary: The government proposes a 5% GST rate for mango drinks with 22-25% real pulp. This move aims to revive Totapuri mango pulp demand and improve beverage quality. An expert committee studied Totapuri price declines and suggested solutions for farmers.

articles.title: What is the full form of BMW? Here's the meaning behind the world famous luxury car brand
articles.summary: BMW's first automobile arrived in 1928 after acquiring Fahrzeugfabrik Eisenach, marking the beginning of its journey in the passenger car industry

articles.title: Bought rare ₹1 notes online? This consumer's court win could matter to every online shopper
articles.summary: The commission observed that Snapdeal acted in a deficient and negligent manner by failing to verify the contents before delivering the parcel

articles.title: Used the wrong birth date for school admission? Delhi HC allows correction but fines parents ₹2 lakh
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court said a child cannot be made to suffer for a parent's wrongdoing, allowing correction of the student's birth date while penalising the parents for using incorrect information during admission.

articles.title: Stock market today: NSE Nifty50 opens below 24,000, BSE Sensex sheds over 350 points
Oil prices touch six week high as Strait of Hormuz is 'completely closed'
articles.summary: Crude oil prices surged as Middle East conflict escalated on Thursday. Brent crude rose above $96 a barrel and WTI climbed past $88. Fresh attacks on oil tankers in the Red Sea fueled supply concerns. Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed control of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Mark Zuckerberg: 'Move fast and break things ...'
articles.summary: The quote highlights the importance of embracing uncertainty, innovation, and bold decision-making in a rapidly evolving world.

articles.title: Under-recoveries push HPCL, BPCL into losses
OpenAI's rogue AI breach may bolster India's case for frontier model access
Q1 bank credit flow surges 10x as market mop-up collapses
Improving liquidity helping robust growth in credit: RBI
articles.summary: India's economy shows resilience amid global uncertainties and fragile geopolitics. Liquidity conditions have improved, supporting robust credit growth across the nation. The external sector remains steady, with foreign investment inflows strengthening its outlook.

articles.title: Pharma companies in a flux over Trump's 200% tariff move on generics
Temasek widens scope of India investments
articles.summary: Temasek is broadening its Indian investment focus beyond traditional sectors. The investor will now target industrials and artificial intelligence opportunities. Its India portfolio has grown significantly, reaching $42 billion by March 2026.

articles.title: Trump's 25% tariff hits Brazil: Trade tensions deepen as billions in exports at risk
'Aam'ezing voyage: UP's mangoes make first sea journey to Dubai
articles.summary: Indian mangoes reached Dubai via a 25-day sea journey. Nearly ninety percent of the fruit remained market-ready after transit. This successful shipment paves the way for cheaper and larger mango exports. The new protocol reduces reliance on expensive air freight for shipments.

articles.title: Trump's 200% tariff plan may be a bitter pill for US consumers; Dr Reddy's warns of costlier medicines
articles.summary: The United States plans significant tariffs on imported generic medicines. These duties will rise to 200% in the coming years. Dr Reddy's Laboratories anticipates higher medicine prices for American consumers.

articles.title: Airline Sent ₹6,437, customer got nothing. Know why Paytm must now pay ₹21,000
articles.summary: The complaint against Go Airlines was dismissed after the commission found that the airline had already transferred the refund amount to Paytm.

articles.title: RBI says India's economy remains resilient despite global uncertainty
articles.summary: India's economy has remained resilient despite heightened global uncertainty, supported by healthy domestic demand and strong industrial and services sector performance, the RBI said in its July 2026 Bulletin.

articles.title: IndusInd Bank: Q1 a clear inflection point; bank well positioned to improve growth in coming quarters, says MD & CEO Rajiv Anand
articles.summary: Accelerating profitable growth across retail, SME (small and medium enterprises), rural and wholesale banking are some of the priorities the private sector lender has set as it looks ahead.

articles.title: US stock market today: Wall Street drifts in mixed trading; oil prices rise another 3%
articles.summary: The S&P 500 slipped 0.2%, despite a majority of its constituent stocks trading higher. Artificial intelligence-related stocks have remained the key driver of recent market volatility.

articles.title: India's household debt rises to 45.5% of GDP; non-housing retail loans account for 58.4%, show data
articles.summary: India's household debt has climbed to 45.5% of GDP, but the bigger concern is not the rise in borrowing—it's the changing nature of credit demand. As consumption loans outpace housing and business loans, experts say the quality of debt deserves as much attention as its size.

articles.title: 'Who will be responsible if we lose our jobs?': Commuters question metro shutdowns amid CJP protest
articles.summary: Passengers also complained that they were receiving little information about the situation, even as slogans were reportedly being raised at the station.

articles.title: Skoda Slavia Monte Carlo gets two rare colours — only 200 cars will be made
articles.summary: Skoda has added Shimla Green and Steel Grey to the Slavia Monte Carlo. Only 200 cars will be offered, all with black roofs and automatic gearboxes.

articles.title: China's global economic rise is 'remarkable'; India overtakes US in world savings: EAC-PM report
articles.summary: Report highlights India’s rise across several parameters, with the country accounting for 10.3% of world savings and 10.8% of investment

articles.title: BPCL posts Rs 3,962 crore Q1 loss as high crude prices squeeze fuel margins
articles.summary: Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd reported a significant net loss for the April-June quarter. Elevated crude oil prices and regulated fuel rates severely impacted marketing margins. The company incurred substantial LPG under-recoveries and unpaid subsidy dues.

articles.title: 'We literally have the paper': US says India trade deal could be signed in months
articles.summary: A significant India-US trade agreement is expected within three to four months. Negotiations are nearly finalized, with the text effectively complete and ready for signing. The primary remaining hurdle involves ongoing US trade investigations, not bilateral disagreements.

articles.title: Running on rollovers: Pakistan's reserves get $27 billion foreign loans from Saudi, China
articles.summary: Pakistan secured approximately twenty-seven billion dollars in foreign funding last fiscal year. Saudi Arabia and China provided significant rollover support for existing loans. Fresh borrowing accounted for sixteen billion dollars of the total inflows.

articles.title: Universal provident fund scheme: How PF for gig workers, self-employed could work & why it would be a game-changer
articles.summary: The proposed framework aims to allow unorganised sector workers and self-employed to set aside a portion of their income for deposits under a universal provident fund scheme which will earn regular interest on par with the existing EPFO scheme.

articles.title: Boiling point? Brent crude touches five week high, here’s what analysts expect next for oil markets
articles.summary: Oil prices are climbing as geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran intensify. Analysts expect crude prices to remain within a specific range for now. Current market prices do not indicate a major structural disruption to global oil supplies.

articles.title: Stock market today: Sensex closes 715 points lower; Nifty ends below 24,000
